steps.title: Compare the work claims
steps.content: Text 1 presents poetry as unusually accessible because poets can write while earning a living another way, even in spare moments between shifts or on the subway. Text 2 presents a different view of contemporary poetry: publishing in major outlets often involves professional training and extensive practice, which require time that people with full-time jobs often do not have. That creates a disagreement about whether full-time work seriously blocks the practice of poetry.

steps.title: Reject questions without conflict
steps.content:
• Choice A asks whether other art forms require time and money, a point Text 1 makes but Text 2 does not dispute.
  • Choice B asks whether poets should gain nonartistic work experience, which neither text addresses.
  • Choice C asks about training and quality, but Text 1 does not discuss professional training and Text 2 focuses on access to time and publication.
The remaining question identifies the direct disagreement about full-time work. The answer is D.
